senses.definition: a physical game that is played on a large plastic mat with colorful circles, where players take turns spinning a wheel to determine which body part and which circle color to place it on, resulting in a tangled web of limbs and bodies as players contort themselves to avoid falling over
a skill-based amusement game where players use a handheld wand to navigate along a twisted wire without touching it, avoiding triggering a buzzing sound

examples.x: She spent the afternoon playing with the wire loop game, trying not to touch the wire.
I was so close to finishing the wire loop game, but I accidentally touched the wire.
The kids took turns playing the wire loop game at the family gathering.
It took a lot of concentration to get through the wire loop game without setting off the buzzer.

senses.definition: a competitive game where players use handheld infrared-emitting devices to tag opponents' targets, simulating combat scenarios in a safe and fun environment

examples.x: We had so much fun playing laser tag at the arcade last weekend.
The kids love going to the laser tag arena for their birthday parties.
I can't wait to join my friends for a game of laser tag after school.
After a long day of work, we decided to unwind with a few rounds of laser tag.
He scored the most points in our laser tag game, and we all congratulated him.

senses.definition: a game for children in which players wrap a string around their fingers and try to make different designs
a physical and mental skill game that was first created in the 1980s, played with a set of 54 wooden blocks, which are stacked in a tower formation at the beginning of the game

a simple game where players take turns trying to toss or roll a penny into a small hole or target, often drawn on the ground or a board, and the player who successfully lands the penny inside the hole wins

examples.x: The children spent the afternoon playing penny in the hole in the backyard.
She explained the rules of penny in the hole to the newcomers before they joined the game.
During the break, the workers played a quick round of penny in the hole for fun.
Penny in the hole is a simple game, but it is a great way to pass time with friends.

senses.definition: a traditional English game where players try to knock a doll named "Aunt Sally" off a stand by throwing sticks or balls

examples.x: We played Aunt Sally at the village fair last weekend and had a great time.
I finally knocked the dolly off the stand in Aunt Sally after a few tries.
Have you ever tried Aunt Sally?
Aunt Sally was one of the main attractions at the festival this year.
The pub had an old-fashioned Aunt Sally set up outside for people to enjoy.

senses.definition: a fun game where players try to toss rings onto a target, typically a peg or a bottle, to score points based on where the rings land

examples.x: She played ring toss at the carnival and won a stuffed animal.
We watched a few people try their luck at ring toss before joining in.
We set up a ring toss game in the backyard for the party.
I always enjoy playing ring toss when we go to the fair.

senses.definition: a traditional pub game where players attempt to swing a ring attached to a string onto a hook or bull's horn fixed to a wall, aiming for accuracy and skill in landing the ring on the target

examples.x: We played ring the bull at the fair and had a great time trying to land the rings.
The kids loved playing ring the bull while their parents enjoyed a snack nearby.
We spent the afternoon at the festival, and ring the bull was one of our favorite games to play.
I tried ring the bull at the carnival, but I couldn’t get a single ring around the peg.
After a few rounds of ring the bull, we decided to move on to another game.

senses.definition: a traditional pub game played on a small table with pockets, combining elements of billiards and bagatelle, providing a social and skill-based pastime in pubs and bars

examples.x: We played bar billiards at the pub last night, and I almost won.
Bar billiards is such a fun game, it’s perfect for a casual evening with friends.
He is really good at bar billiards, always scoring high points.
She spent hours at the pub playing bar billiards with her friends.
After a few rounds of bar billiards, we decided to grab a drink and relax.

senses.definition: a game in which small pointy objects are thrown at a board to achieve points

examples.x: We played darts at the pub last night and I won three games in a row.
I’ve been practicing darts every day to improve my aim.
The kids had fun playing darts at the family gathering, though they were using plastic darts.
She spent the afternoon watching darts on TV, cheering for her favorite player.
They set up a darts competition for the office party, and everyone participated.

senses.definition: a table game where players slide weighted pucks to score points in scoring zones, played for fun and competition

examples.x: We spent the afternoon playing shuffleboard at the beach resort.

senses.definition: an ancient game played with small objects, typically sheep's knucklebones or metal jacks, where players toss and pick up the pieces in various sequences, providing a traditional and challenging pastime

examples.x: She played knucklebones with her friends during recess.
I remember playing knucklebones in the park when I was young.
The game of knucklebones was popular in ancient times.
He taught me how to play knucklebones using small pebbles.
They spent the afternoon playing knucklebones, laughing and competing.

senses.definition: a classic game played with a set of thin sticks or straws, where players take turns trying to remove sticks from a pile without disturbing the others, providing a fun and strategic challenge

examples.x: We played pick-up sticks after dinner, and it was a lot of fun.
She was really good at pick-up sticks and always managed to get the most.
It was a quiet afternoon, so we decided to sit down and play pick-up sticks.
He challenged me to a game of pick-up sticks, and I lost on my first turn.

senses.definition: a table game resembling football in which players attempt to score goals by turning side handles that have figures of football players attached to them

examples.x: My friends and I often play foosball during our lunch breaks at work.
I’ve never been great at foosball, but I enjoy playing with my family.
He scored the winning goal in foosball with just seconds left on the clock.
She challenged me to a foosball match, and I couldn’t turn it down.

senses.definition: a tabletop game in which two players use paddles to hit a puck back and forth on a low-friction surface, attempting to score goals in each other's net

examples.x: We played air hockey at the arcade and had so much fun.
We spent the afternoon at the mall playing air hockey and eating snacks.
My brother is really good at air hockey; he always wins when we play.
We set up an air hockey tournament with our friends this weekend.
She scored a goal with an amazing shot during our air hockey match.

senses.definition: a classic arcade game in which players roll balls up a ramp to try to land them in scoring holes of varying point values

examples.x: I had a great time at the arcade playing skee-ball with my friends.
After dinner, we went to the arcade and played some skee-ball to see who could win the most tickets.
I used to love playing skee-ball when I was a kid at the carnival.
She scored 100 points on her first try at skee-ball.

senses.definition: a hand game where two players simultaneously form one of three shapes with their hands determining the winner based on simple rules: rock crushes scissors, scissors cuts paper, and paper covers rock

examples.x: Let's play rock-paper-scissors to decide who goes first.
Whenever my friends can't agree on something, we use rock-paper-scissors to settle it.
He won the game with rock-paper-scissors, choosing scissors to cut my paper.
I always lose at rock-paper-scissors when I choose paper.
We ended up playing rock-paper-scissors to decide who would carry the heavy box.

senses.definition: a game that involves rhythmic hand movements and clapping, often accompanied by a song or rhyme, and typically played by two or more players taking turns to perform specific hand gestures and claps in sync with the rhythm

examples.x: We played a hand clapping game during recess and laughed the whole time.
She taught me a new hand clapping game that her friends used to play.
They were having so much fun with the hand clapping game that they didn’t even notice the time passing.
I remember playing a hand clapping game with my cousins every summer when we were kids.
At school, the girls often challenge each other to a hand clapping game to see who can keep the rhythm the longest.

senses.definition: a hand game where players try to slide their fingers into certain positions or patterns, following the leader's actions

a hand game where two players simultaneously show either an odd number of fingers or an even number of fingers, and the winner is determined based on the sum of the fingers shown by both players, providing a simple and fun game of chance and decision-making

examples.x: We couldn't decide who should go first, so we played odds and evens.
Let's use odds and evens to pick who gets the last slice of pizza.
Do you want to play odds and evens to see who gets the remote?
We kept playing odds and evens every time we disagreed on something.

senses.definition: a hand game where two players interlock their thumbs and try to pin down their opponent's thumb for a count of three, similar to traditional wrestling but using only the thumbs

examples.x: We decided to settle the argument with a quick thumb war.
After school, we always play thumb war to see who gets to choose the game.
I won the thumb war, so I get the last slice of pizza!
They were laughing so hard during the thumb war that they could barely keep their hands still.

senses.definition: a hand-clapping game usually accompanied by a rhyme, where two players clap their hands together in a pattern while reciting the rhyme

examples.x: The children were laughing as they played pat-a-cake together.
My daughter loves when I play pat-a-cake with her before bedtime.
She taught her little brother how to do pat-a-cake while they sat on the floor.
During the playdate, they spent hours playing pat-a-cake and singing the rhyme.
The toddler giggled as we played pat-a-cake, trying to mimic the clapping pattern.

senses.definition: a game where players use hand gestures to answer questions or perform dares, often used in conjunction with spoken questions

examples.x: We played truth or dare at the party and ended up laughing the whole night.
He picked "truth" in the game of truth or dare and ended up revealing a funny secret.
We sat in a circle, ready to start a game of truth or dare.
Truth or dare can get a bit wild, especially when the dares get more creative.
At the sleepover, truth or dare was the highlight of the night.
